stories.title: Tennessee 3-1 Georgia — Pickens Strikes Out 10 in Super Regional Opener (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Karlyn Pickens was untouchable last night.

10 strikeouts. Complete game. Just 1 run allowed.

Tennessee beats Georgia 3-1 in Super Regional Game 1. 🥎

Game 2 is TODAY at 3 PM ET on ESPN2. #LadyVols

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Knight. Clarke. Leach.

Three solo shots. That's all Tennessee needed.

Pickens did the rest — 10 Ks, complete game, 3-1 win.

Game 2 today at 3 PM ET, ESPN2. One win from the WCWS. #LadyVols #GoDawgs

facebook.long_form:
🥎 KARLYN PICKENS DID THAT 🥎

Tennessee is one win away from the Women's College World Series.

Last night in Knoxville, Karlyn Pickens walked out to the circle and shut Georgia down. Complete game. Ten strikeouts. One run allowed — a solo home run from Emily Digby in the second inning. That was all the Bulldogs got.

Three Tennessee home runs did the work at the plate — solo shots from Sophia Knight, Emma Clarke, and Alannah Leach. Three up, three over the fence. 3-1 final.

Georgia came into Knoxville with momentum. Addisen Fisher had just thrown a complete-game shutout in the regional final. The Bulldogs were confident. And Pickens answered every question they brought with 10 strikeouts and no answers left for them.

Game 2 is TODAY at 3 PM ET on ESPN2.

Tennessee is one game from Devon Park. Pickens will be watching from the dugout, or she'll be back in the circle. Either way, the Lady Vols are in the driver's seat.

🔵 Game 2 | Friday, May 22 | 3 PM ET | ESPN2 | Knoxville

[Are you watching Game 2 today? Drop a 🥎 below!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Karlyn Pickens: 10 strikeouts, complete game, 1 run. Tennessee takes Super Regional Game 1 over Georgia, 3-1. One win from Devon Park OKC. Game 2 today at 3 PM ET on ESPN2. 🥎 #LadyVols

stories.title: Wells Enters Super Regional — OU vs. Miss State Game 1 at 1 PM ET (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells. 37 home runs. Tied with Espinoza (Arizona, 1995).

The Super Regional starts NOW in Norman.

Oklahoma vs. Mississippi State — 1 PM ET, ESPN2.

Every at-bat could be history. 🥎 #Sooners

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Mississippi State's problem today:

Pitch to Wells and risk history.

Walk her and Oklahoma's lineup destroys you.

There's no safe answer. That's what it means to face the most dangerous hitter in college softball. 🥎 #Sooners

facebook.long_form:
🥎 THE MOST ANTICIPATED AT-BATS IN COLLEGE SOFTBALL — TODAY 🥎

Kendall Wells enters the Super Regional with 37 home runs.

That ties the single-season mark set by Laura Espinoza of Arizona in 1995. Thirty-one years, and that number has stood. One swing separates Wells from making it her own.

Today, Oklahoma hosts Mississippi State in Super Regional Game 1 at 1 PM ET on ESPN2 at Love's Field in Norman.

Mississippi State's pitching staff has had a week to think about this. How do you pitch to the most dangerous hitter in college softball? There's no clean answer. Walk her and you face the rest of an Oklahoma lineup that has been destroying pitching all season. Attack the zone and you might be the one who ends up serving up history.

Wells isn't chasing the record. She's chasing the WCWS. The record is what happens when you put up a season like she has.

Every at-bat today will carry weight. Every pitch will matter. This is why we watch college softball.

🔶 Game 1 | Friday, May 22 | 1 PM ET | ESPN2 | Norman, OK

[Who do you think pitches to Wells today — or do you think Miss State walks her every time?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: 37 home runs. One from history. Kendall Wells opens Super Regional play TODAY vs. Mississippi State — 1 PM ET, ESPN2 in Norman. Every at-bat is must-watch. 🥎 #Sooners

stories.title: Nebraska's Super Regional Resumes TODAY at 4 PM CT After Weather Delay (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Rain delayed it. Didn't cancel it.

Nebraska's first-ever home Super Regional resumes TODAY at 4 PM CT.

Frahm vs. Neylan. Sold-out Bob Devaney. 13 years since the WCWS.

Lincoln still gets its night. 🌽 #Huskers #okstate

facebook.long_form:
🌽 RAIN TRIED TO STOP NEBRASKA. IT FAILED. 🌽

Thursday night in Lincoln, the build-up was everything you'd expect.

Bob Devaney Sports Center was sold out. Nebraska (49-6) was about to play their first-ever home Super Regional. Jordy Frahm — a Lincoln native, who grew up dreaming of this exact moment — was ready to pitch on the biggest night in Nebraska softball history.

And then it rained. Lightning struck. Twenty-three minutes into Game 1, play stopped. Nebraska had a runner on first with one out in the bottom of the first inning. The crowd waited. The announcement came: postponed.

But this morning, the announcement that matters: Game 1 resumes TODAY at 4 PM CT.

Everything that made last night incredible is still there. The sold-out crowd. Frahm on the mound. Ruby Neylan of Oklahoma State — an Omaha native who once played travel ball with Frahm, now wearing Cowgirl colors — waiting to face her.

Nebraska's 13-year WCWS drought. Their first-ever home Super Regional. All of it still on the line.

The rain didn't stop Lincoln. It just made them wait a little longer.

🌽 Game 1 (Resumed) | Friday, May 22 | 4 PM CT | Lincoln, NE

[Drop a 🌽 if you're back and ready to watch today!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Rain delayed it — didn't cancel it. Nebraska's first-ever home Super Regional resumes TODAY at 4 PM CT. Frahm vs. Neylan. Bob Devaney sold out. 13 years of waiting. Lincoln still gets its night. 🌽 #Huskers

stories.title: Alabama vs. LSU Game 1 Tonight at 7 PM ET — No. 1 Meets No. 16 (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
No. 1 Alabama vs. No. 16 LSU — tonight at 7 PM ET, ESPN2.

The Tide (52-7) bring Moten and Briski.

LSU just outscored 3 regional opponents by 21 runs.

On paper: blowout. On the field: watch and see. 🌙 #RollTide #LSU

facebook.long_form:
🏟️ THE BIGGEST SEED GAP IN THE TOURNAMENT — TONIGHT IN TUSCALOOSA 🏟️

No. 1 Alabama. No. 16 LSU. Same field. Same stakes.

On paper, this is a mismatch. Alabama (52-7) enters this Super Regional as the most dominant program in the country, with a pitching staff that was nearly untouchable in the regional. Vic Moten was close to perfect. Briski provides depth. The Crimson Tide have been to 20 Super Regionals — an NCAA record.

But LSU just outscored their three regional opponents 23-2. That is not a team that backed into this Super Regional.

These two programs did not meet during the regular season. Alabama's pitching staff doesn't have a specific scouting advantage. LSU's offense doesn't have any data on what Moten does in close situations. In a tournament where matchup familiarity matters, this one starts completely fresh.

The No. 16 seed hasn't beaten the No. 1 seed yet this weekend. But tonight at 7 PM ET on ESPN2 in Tuscaloosa, LSU gets their chance.

🔴 Game 1 | Friday, May 22 | 7 PM ET | ESPN2 | Tuscaloosa, AL

[Are you picking the upset, or does Alabama run away with it?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: No. 1 Alabama (52-7) vs. No. 16 LSU (40-17) — tonight at 7 PM ET on ESPN2. LSU just outscored three opponents by 21 runs. Alabama's Moten is near-perfect. Something has to give. 🌙 #RollTide #LSU

stories.title: UCF at UCLA Tonight — Cinderella Rides Again at 9 PM ET (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
UCF already beat No. 9 FSU in Tallahassee.

Now they fly across the country to face No. 8 UCLA.

The Cinderella story doesn't stop at the state line.

9 PM ET, ESPNU. This is must-watch softball. 🏰🥎 #UCF

facebook.long_form:
🏰 UCF IS NOT DONE YET 🏰

Let's recap what UCF has done this postseason.

They won their regional without losing a game.

Then they went to Tallahassee — No. 9 FSU's home field — and beat Florida State 4-2 in the regional final. UCF's first-ever NCAA win over FSU. It wasn't close at the end.

Now they're getting on a plane and flying across the country to play No. 8 UCLA at Easton Stadium in Los Angeles.

This UCF team doesn't seem to care about road trips. They don't seem to care about seedings. They beat FSU on their home field. That's the kind of team that earns the right to be called a real threat in a Super Regional.

UCLA is a great team. Home field is real. Senior experience is real. But UCF has now proven — multiple times this postseason — that the "better team on paper" doesn't automatically win.

Tonight at 9 PM ET on ESPNU, the Cinderella story either continues or ends.

We'll be watching. You should be too.

🏰 Game 1 | Friday, May 22 | 9 PM ET | ESPNU | Los Angeles, CA

[Are you riding with UCF? Or does UCLA end it here?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: UCF beat No. 9 FSU in Tallahassee. Now they head to L.A. to face No. 8 UCLA. The Cinderella story doesn't stop at the state line. Game 1 tonight at 9 PM ET on ESPNU. 🏰🥎 #UCF

stories.title: Arkansas's WCWS Drought on the Line — Duke Opens Today at 11 AM ET (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Arkansas has been to 5 Super Regionals.

They have never made it to the WCWS.

Today at 11 AM ET on ESPNU, they face Duke — a program with nothing to lose.

This is Courtney Deifel's biggest game. It starts now. 🥎 #Razorbacks

facebook.long_form:
🐗 FIVE SUPER REGIONALS. ZERO WCWS TRIPS. TODAY IS DIFFERENT. 🐗

Courtney Deifel has built Arkansas softball into a powerhouse.

She's also been to 5 Super Regionals and never reached the Women's College World Series. That's not a knock — it's context. It means every time Arkansas gets here, there's unfinished business. There's a weight that comes with the history of not getting over the hump.

Today at 11 AM ET on ESPNU, Arkansas hosts Duke at Bogle Park in Fayetteville.

Duke has been to 4 Super Regionals in the last 5 years. They know this stage. They know how to come into an opponent's home field and play without the crowd getting to them. And with the narrative pressure sitting entirely on Arkansas's shoulders, the Blue Devils have the most dangerous thing in sports: nothing to lose.

But Arkansas is at home. Bogle Park will be electric. And Deifel's program is good enough to win this series — they've always been good enough. This year might finally be the year the results match the reputation.

Game 1 is today. The answer starts now.

🐗 Game 1 | Friday, May 22 | 11 AM ET | ESPNU | Fayetteville, AR

[Arkansas fans — is THIS finally the year? Tell us below!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Courtney Deifel is 0-for-5 in Super Regionals. Today at 11 AM ET on ESPNU, Arkansas hosts Duke in their most emotionally loaded game of the season. Five years of heartbreak — does it end today? 🐗 #Razorbacks
